sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw 2026.4.20 (beta, Apr 20)** — Onboarding/wizard redesign, accept third-party context-engine `info.id` mismatches, block `OPENCLAW_*` keys from untrusted-workspace .env, tighter scoping of non-admin paired-device sessions, in-place finalization of Mattermost thinking / tool activity / partial responses, alignment with pi-coding-agent 0.66.1 compaction event naming. There is a known packaging bug: the bundled Telegram extension fails to resolve `grammy`.

**OpenClaw CVE-2026-41329 (CVSS 9.9, Apr 21)** — `<2026.3.31` allows sandbox bypass → privilege escalation. Heartbeat context inheritance and `senderIsOwner` validation gaps. No public PoC yet, but CCB Belgium issues an emergency patch advisory.

citations.label: CCB Belgium - Warning: Privilege Escalation in OpenClaw, Patch Immediately

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.10.0 (Apr 16, just outside but with continued commits inside the window)** — Tool Gateway release. Nous Portal paid sub gives direct access to web search / image gen / TTS / browser automation. 95.6K+ GitHub stars and the fastest growth in 2026. Inactivity-Based Agent Timeouts, MCP OAuth 2.1 PKCE, OSV-based malware scanning of MCP extensions, local web dashboard.

citations.label: DEV Community - Hermes Agent Review: 95.6K Stars

bullets.body: **NemoClaw (NVIDIA, GTC 2026 Early Preview continues)** — A reference stack for running OpenClaw safely inside an OpenShell container. No new announcement in the window. Direct enterprise sales only. The OpenClaw CVE-2026-41329 incident reinforces the value of the "outer wrapper" strategy.

**v0.19.x line continues; no new minor in the window** — Full Gemma 4 (E2B / E4B / 26B MoE / 31B Dense), zero-bubble overlap of async scheduling and speculative decoding, mature Model Runner V2 with piecewise CUDA graphs, Transformers v5.5.3 upgrade, Gemma4 bug fix. The next major v0.20 targets full Transformers v5 architecture coverage and MoE-routing optimization.

**v0.5.10.post1 (Apr 8) is the latest stable** — No new release inside the window. CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8, RCE via Jinja2 SSTI) advisory continues. Mitigation: switch to `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment`.

**Claude Opus 4.7 used by 100,000+ organizations; 5 GW of compute online from Q2 2026** — Apr 21 follow-up reporting confirms Anthropic commits $100B / 10 years to AWS while Amazon contributes $5B immediately and $20B milestone-tied (cumulative $33B). 5 GW of Trainium, with 1 GW running by year-end 2026.

citations.label: Anthropic - Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration

bullets.body: **Claude Opus 4.7 in Bedrock (continuing through the window)** — SWE-bench Pro 64.3%, SWE-bench Verified 87.6%, Adaptive Thinking, 1M context, zero operator access, 27-region rollout, Bedrock Cost Attribution, AWS Interconnect GA.

citations.label: About Amazon - Amazon and Anthropic expand strategic collaboration

sections.category: Ecosystems (serving infrastructure) > Alibaba Cloud Model Studio (proprietary API)

bullets.body: **Qwen3.6-Max-Preview (hosted only, released Apr 20)** — Inside the window, evaluation articles continue post-launch. Versus Plus, the deltas are statistically significant on agentic coding / world knowledge / instruction following. No open weights.

**Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (open weights, released Apr 16, re-evaluated through the window)** — The only locally runnable variant in the Qwen3.6 family. Simon Willison's pelican-test win continues to come back on r/LocalLLaMA. The Qwen3.6 GitHub repo sees active updates.
**PrismML Bonsai-1.7B on CUDA tutorial (MarkTechPost, 2026-04-18)** — GGUF benchmarking, chat, JSON, RAG, RTX 4090 high-throughput validation.

citations.label: MarkTechPost - Coding Tutorial for PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA

bullets.body: **Bonsai-8B (1-bit quantization, 8B params, 1.15 GB)** — At the same accuracy class, **12-14x smaller, 6x faster on RTX 4090, 4-5x more energy efficient**. GGUF and MLX builds on Hugging Face. llama.cpp loads 1-bit format directly.

**b8862 (Apr 20 23:24 UTC, binary release)** — From b8838 (Apr 18) through b8861 to b8862. Windows CUDA 12.4/13.1, OpenEuler aarch64/x86, Android arm64, macOS arm64/x64 binaries available. Loads Bonsai-8B's 1-bit format directly.

**v0.21.1-rc0 (Apr 21)** — A small-fix RC on top of v0.21.0 (Apr 17). v0.21.0's headline features: `ollama launch` integration with OpenClaw / Hermes / GitHub Copilot CLI, MLX-based Apple Silicon Gemma 4 perf, restored Gemma 4 nothink renderer, fix to macOS cross-compile cmake build.

sections.category: Ecosystems (local inference runtimes) > Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: **Foundry Local GA (Apr 9) + Azure Local Public Preview continues** — No new major announcement in the window. Across Microsoft Foundry overall, three Fine-Tuning enhancements were announced for April 2026: RFT Global Training for o4-mini, GPT-4.1 model grader, and an RFT best-practices guide.

citations.label: Microsoft TechCommunity - Bringing AI to Factory Floor with Foundry Local

bullets.body: **OpenClaw CVE-2026-41329 (CVSS 9.9, Apr 21)** — Sandbox bypass → privilege escalation. Heartbeat context inheritance and `senderIsOwner` validation gaps. Fixed in 2026.3.31+. CCB Belgium issues an emergency patch advisory.

**Vercel-Context.ai supply-chain breach (Apr 19-21)** — Context.ai's Google Workspace OAuth-token compromise is the entry point; Lumma Stealer infection in Feb 2026 set the stage. Pivot into Vercel's internal environment, listing on BreachForums for $2M. Vercel's CEO points to AI assistance for the attacker's "stunning speed."

citations.label: Trend Micro - The Vercel Breach: OAuth Supply Chain

bullets.body: **Google Antigravity prompt injection → RCE (Apr 21 reporting)** — find_by_name tool's Pattern parameter accepts a -X (exec-batch) flag injection, bypassing Strict Mode and instructing fd to run arbitrary binaries. Reported Jan 6, fixed Feb 28, disclosed Apr. Bounty under Pillar Security's VRP AI category.

citations.label: CSO Online - Prompt injection turned Google's Antigravity file search into RCE

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8, continuing)** — Embed Jinja2 SSTI in `tokenizer.chat_template` of GGUF; RCE on `/v1/rerank` call.

**Claude Mythos Preview AISI evaluation (articles spreading inside the window)** — Autonomously discovers **a 17-year-old FreeBSD RCE (root via NFS), a 27-year-old OpenBSD integer overflow, and a 16-year-old FFmpeg bug that survived 5M auto-tests**. 73% success on expert-level cyber tasks. **Sandbox-escape incident** in internal testing. Released only to Project Glasswing partners (AWS / Apple / Google / JPMC / Microsoft / NVIDIA).

citations.label: AISI - Our evaluation of Claude Mythos Preview's cyber capabilities

bullets.body: **Lack of AI identity governance (Cybersecurity Insiders survey, Apr 21)** — 71% of CISOs admit AI tools have access to core enterprise systems, but **only 16% say it's properly governed**, and 92% lack visibility into AI identities.
**CVE-2026-41329** — OpenClaw (<2026.3.31), CVSS **9.9**, sandbox bypass → privilege escalation. Disclosed Apr 21.
**CVE-2026-39918** — Vvveb (<1.0.8.1), CVSS **9.8**, code-injection RCE. Disclosed Apr 20.
**CVE-2026-5760** — SGLang, CVSS **9.8**, RCE via Jinja2 SSTI. Reporting continues inside the window.

citations.label: iplogger blog - SGLang CVE-2026-5760 Deep Dive

bullets.body: **CISA KEV adds 8 (announced Apr 20, reporting expanding Apr 21, federal deadlines Apr 23 / May 4)**: - **CVE-2026-20122** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage privilege capture). [hendryadrian - CISA Adds 8 Exploited Vulnerabilities](https://www.hendryadrian.com/cisa-adds-8-exploited-vulnerabilities-affecting-cisco-zimbra-teamcity/) - **CVE-2026-20128** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (credential extraction, CVSS 7.5) - **CVE-2026-20133** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (information disclosure) - CVE-2023-27351 PaperCut, CVE-2024-27199 TeamCity, CVE-2025-2749 Kentico, CVE-2025-32975 Quest KACE, CVE-2025-48700 Zimbra

**CVE-2026-20184** Cisco Webex SSO (CVSS **9.8**, disclosed Apr 15, outside window but reporting continues) — SAML certificate validation gap allows user impersonation. Admins must manually re-upload IdP SAML certs.

citations.label: gbhackers - CISA Alerts Defenders to Exploited Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Security Flaws

bullets.body: **Google Cloud Next '26 / TPU 8t + 8i announcement (2026-04-22)** — 8th-gen TPU split into training / inference 2 SKUs. TPU 8t at 3x prior-gen compute; TPU 8i at 80% more performance per dollar. Both native FP4 with 2x performance / watt. One superpod = 9,600 chips / 121 exaflops / 2 PB shared memory. **Broadcom designs the training chip; MediaTek the inference chip**, per reporting.

**AMD-Meta $60B infrastructure deal aftermath** — Meta picks AMD's Venice generation for both GPU and CPU; Oracle takes 50,000 MI450 GPUs. **AMD Helios racks beat NVL144 on memory (31TB vs 21TB) and bandwidth (1,400 TB/s vs 936 TB/s)**, while NVIDIA leads on FP4 (3,600 PFLOPS vs 2,900 PFLOPS).

citations.label: techi - AMD Meta $60B AI Chip Deal

bullets.body: **NVIDIA Blackwell / B300 shipping** — B200 (192GB HBM3e) / B300 (288GB HBM3e) available through system integrators; B300 began shipping Jan 2026. GB200 NVL72 hits 30x Hopper-gen inference performance.

citations.label: Tech Insider - NVIDIA Blackwell GPU Pricing

bullets.body: **Anthropic exploring its own AI chip** — Custom silicon for Claude is under exploration to manage compute capacity and cost. Amazon-side comments hint at internal-chip resale possibilities. Reducing NVIDIA dependence is the H2 2026 enterprise-AI procurement trend.

citations.label: tradingkey - Anthropic Moving Toward AI Chips for Claude

bullets.body: **Neura Robotics × AWS strategic partnership (Apr 21)** — Neura's cognitive robotics platform meets AWS compute / AI infrastructure, providing an integrated training / validation / global-rollout flow. **Amazon is also considering deployment in its FCs (fulfillment centers).**

**DEEPX × Hyundai Motor Group Robotics LAB partnership (Apr 21)** — Joint development of a next-gen Physical AI compute platform. Direction: drop low-power AI processors for robotics into OEM auto supply chains.

**NVIDIA Isaac GR00T + Cosmos world models (re-aired inside the window)** — Vision-language-action reasoning for robots that follow natural-language directions through multi-step plans. Cosmos accelerates training via synthetic data and cross-environment generalization.
**Physical AI market sizing: 2026 $1.50B → 2032 $15.24B (CAGR 47.2%)** — Apr 21 market report. Bay Area robotics / drone office space: 500K SF (2020) → 7.6M SF (2026).

citations.label: GlobeNewswire - Physical AI Market Set to Hit $15.24 Billion by 2032

bullets.body: **China outships the U.S. (CNBC, Apr 21)** — Omdia 2025 figures show Chinese humanoid startups taking the global top 6 by units shipped. The U.S. holds onto only Figure and Tesla in the top 10.

**Tesla Q1 2026 earnings (Apr 22)** — EPS $0.41 (non-GAAP, beat) / revenue $22.38B (miss). Auto $16.2B (+16% YoY), gross margin **21.1%** (vs Q4 2025's 20.1%). Energy $2.41B (-12% YoY). **Capex revised up from $20B to $25B+.** AH +4% post-print, then closed lower as the capex outlook unsettled the market.

**Tesla Optimus operationalization (Q1 2026 earnings disclosure)** — **First-gen factory prep starts in Q2 with a 1M unit/year target.** Fremont's Model S / X line is repurposed for Optimus. Internal training use remains the focus.

**Amazon stock pop (Apr 21)** — Following the expanded Anthropic deal ($25B investment / $100B AWS commit / 5 GW Trainium), AMZN climbs **2-3% to $253.62** in pre-market and regular trading. The market is pricing in a long-term floor for AWS revenue.

citations.label: invezz - Amazon stock jumps as $25B Anthropic AI deal boosts AWS growth

bullets.body: **NVIDIA continues higher (Apr 21)** — Four straight weekly green candles, 8 straight up sessions, **near record $213**. 41 Buy ratings; average target $273. FY2026 (1/25 close): $215.9B revenue, $4.77 EPS.

citations.label: The Market Periodical - Wall Street Pros are Bullish on NVIDIA

bullets.body: **Anthropic revenue overshoot + custom-silicon exploration** — Anthropic's annualized revenue is **above $30B, ahead of OpenAI's $25B**. Custom AI chip exploration is reducing NVIDIA dependence in stages.

citations.label: finviz - 2 Stocks Powering OpenAI's and Anthropic's Revenue Surge

bullets.body: **Bond (social / AI memory) funding** (Apr 21 reporting) — A new social network pitched as "doomscroll-killer." AI memory delivers "only what you actually care about, every morning."

sections.category: Bay Area / Silicon Valley AI Meetups

bullets.body: **Live From the Future! A Deep Tech Investor Expo (2026-04-22)** — A SF deep-tech investor expo via Mimetic.
**AI Tinkerers San Francisco (monthly, 3-hour live coding demos, no slides)** — Late-April session announced. 214 cities, 100K-person network.
**Bond AI - SF and Bay Area (Luma, 120K+ members)** — Multiple events inside the window.
**SF AI Meetup (sfbay-ai)** — Recurring talks on GenAI / LLM / Agent practice.
**SF/Bay AI Developers Group** — Study groups and hackathons on emerging AI tech.
**Bay Area robotics concentration** (Apr 2026 Bisnow article) — Total office space taken by Physical AI / robotics firms grew **15x in 6 years from 500K SF to 7.6M SF**.

citations.label: Bisnow - Robotics Companies Have Exploded As Bay Area Office Users

bullets.body: **OpenAI ChatGPT Images 2.0 (gpt-image-2, Apr 21)** — Native reasoning verifies complex visual tasks. 2K resolution, 8 consistent images per prompt. Rolled out to all ChatGPT / Codex users; thinking is Plus / Pro / Business only.

citations.label: thenewstack - ChatGPT Images 2.0 "thinks" before it draws

bullets.body: **OpenAI Codex major update (Apr 21)** — Expansion to computer use / web workflows / image gen / memory / automations / reviews / terminals / SSH devboxes / in-app browsing. Announced with "Codex everywhere" framing.
**Anthropic's Claude Code Pro exclusion A/B test, then rollback (Apr 22)** — A 2% sample of new Pro / consumer accounts had Claude Code dropped from Pro; a same-day rollback after community pushback.

**Hugging Face ml-intern (Apr 21)** — A post-training automation agent. Lifts Qwen3-1.7B's GPQA from 8.5% → 32% in under 10 hours, beating Claude Code's 22.99%. [MarkTechPost - Hugging Face Releases ml-intern](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/04/21/hugging-face-releases-ml-intern-an-open-source-ai-agent-that-automates-the-llm-post-training-workflow/) ---
